assertions.evidence: Response clearly explains that 'feat' commits trigger MINOR version bumps and are for new backward-compatible features with multiple examples (OAuth 2.0, pagination, full-text search)
Response explains that 'fix' commits trigger PATCH version bumps for bug fixes with concrete examples (race condition, null handling, interface corrections)
Response clearly states that 'chore' commits do NOT trigger version bumps and lists appropriate use cases
Response provides 5 real-world examples (feat, fix, perf, breaking change, chore) with full commit structures and explanations
Response includes decision tree, detailed 'when to use' sections for each type, team tips, and scenario walkthroughs
